title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCCLXXVIII. Love And Matrimony.
content:
There was a little maid, and she was afraid,
        That her sweetheart would come unto her;
        So she went to bed, and cover'd up her head
        And fasten'd the door with a skewer.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. CCCCLXXVIII. Love And Matrimony.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
